human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for banking-operations specificity, execution-state discipline, approval-control posture, fraud and AML escalation safety, disclosure handling, and validated completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to banking account and transaction execution, exception handling, reconciliation, and completion evidence rather than customer support, fraud adjudication, credit, underwriting, accounting signoff, or legal interpretation.

Authority sources cover Federal Reserve workflow rules, CFPB consumer-protection rules, FFIEC and FinCEN suspicious-activity and recordkeeping guidance, and FDIC plus recognized standards for controls and reconciliation posture.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal execution, incomplete inputs, blocked upstream approvals, unsupported overrides, reconciliation conflicts, fraud and AML escalation, disclosure conflicts, boundary rejection, source conflicts, tenant adaptation, and closure-evidence refusal.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/financial-services/banking-operations-specialist/ so banking operations work remains distinct from adjacent fraud, AML, legal, servicing, underwriting, complaints, and accounting lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around unsupported overrides, completion without evidence, fraud or AML escalation, disclosure-sensitive handling, and source-conflict escalation.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated banking operations specialist for evidence-backed account, transaction, exception, and completion handling.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
